name: join
context: Combine pandas DataFrames with merge, join, concat, cardinality validation, suffixes, indicators, and unmatched-row audits
info: Joining is where pandas workflows most often create silent data errors through many-to-many row multiplication, mismatched key dtypes, duplicate keys, and unexpected null-key behavior. Agents use this node to choose `merge`, `join`, or `concat`, validate cardinality with `validate=`, inspect unmatched rows with `indicator=True`, align dtypes before combining, and preserve columns with suffixes. The shared `join_datasets.py` script provides a deterministic file-to-file merge path for CSV, JSON, Excel, and Parquet inputs.
triggers: why did merge create more rows
body:
# Joining DataFrames

Use `merge` for relational joins, `join` for index-oriented joins, and `concat` for stacking rows or columns. Always inspect keys before merging. For SQL-shaped joins, compare [[database/sql]] and [[duckdb/sql]].

## Safe merge

```python
left['user_id'] = left['user_id'].astype('string')
right['user_id'] = right['user_id'].astype('string')

joined = left.merge(
    right,
    on='user_id',
    how='left',
    validate='many_to_one',
    indicator=True,
    suffixes=('', '_user'),
)

unmatched = joined.loc[joined['_merge'].eq('left_only')]
```

## Concatenate

```python
all_months = pd.concat([jan, feb, mar], ignore_index=True)
wide = pd.concat([features, labels], axis=1)
```

## CLI join

```bash
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/join_datasets.py users.csv orders.csv joined.parquet --on user_id --how left --validate one_to_many --indicator
```

sections.title: Merge vs join vs concat
sections.body: `merge` is the general relational join API for columns or indexes. `join` is convenient for index-based joins. `concat` stacks objects along rows or columns and does not match relational keys unless the index is meaningful. Use `ignore_index=True` when stacking row partitions that should get a fresh RangeIndex.

sections.title: Cardinality validation
sections.body: Use `validate='one_to_one'`, `one_to_many`, `many_to_one`, or `many_to_many` to make row multiplication explicit. Before a many-to-one merge, assert the right key is unique. Before one-to-one, assert both sides are unique. If validation fails, inspect duplicate keys with `df[df.duplicated(keys, keep=False)]`.

sections.title: Unmatched row audit
sections.body: Pass `indicator=True` to add `_merge` with `left_only`, `right_only`, or `both`. Count these values after outer or left joins to detect lost records. Keep an unmatched report when the join feeds financial, billing, or compliance outputs.

sections.title: Key hygiene
sections.body: Normalize join key dtypes and whitespace before merging. Treat IDs as strings to preserve leading zeros. Beware null keys; decide whether missing keys should be excluded before the merge. Use suffixes to make overlapping non-key columns explicit.

depends.reason: Join keys should be cleaned before merging
